title: 2013 forty billion dollar share repurchase authorization
decisionQuestion: Should the company authorize a new share repurchase program up to $40 billion?
situation: The board of directors is considering approving a new share repurchase program.
conditions: The board of directors must approve the program.
The authorization limit is set at $40 billion.
constraints: The action must reflect a continued commitment to shareholders.
The decision is being made on September 17, 2013.

options.description: Authorize the new share repurchase program up to $40 billion.
options.expectedBenefit: Demonstrates commitment to returning cash to shareholders.
options.principalRisk: Potential market reaction to the large capital deployment.

options.description: Do not authorize the new share repurchase program.
options.expectedBenefit: Retain capital for other strategic uses.
options.principalRisk: Perception of reduced commitment to shareholder returns.

chosenAction: Authorize the new share repurchase program up to $40 billion.
rejectedBenefit: Retaining capital for alternative strategic investments.
observations: The board approved a new share repurchase program authorizing up to $40 billion.
Amy Hood stated these actions reflect a continued commitment to returning cash to shareholders.
inferences: Authorizing the buyback strongly signals the company's dedication to shareholder returns.
Not authorizing the buyback might suggest a shift away from prioritizing shareholder cash returns.

evidenceSpans.exactQuote: The board of directors also approved a new share repurchase program authorizing up to $40 billion in share repurchases.
These actions reflect a continued commitment to returning cash to our shareholders
evidenceSpans.speaker: Amy Hood
